macro_summary: Markets extended their recovery Tuesday with SPY gaining 1.17% to $694.46 and QQQ up 1.64% to $628.60, as VIX fell 4% to 18.36 — the lowest reading in weeks. The MAS released its April 2026 monetary policy statement on April 14, maintaining the rate of appreciation of the S$NEER policy band, signalling continued measured tightening in Singapore. Per U.S. Bank and Yahoo Finance, the Federal Reserve holds rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% with only one cut projected for 2026, while Chicago Fed President Goolsbee noted that the longer the Iran war persists, the more rate cuts get pushed out. ASML reported earnings today (April 15), raising its 2026 revenue outlook on strong AI demand per Reuters and the Wall Street Journal — a positive read-through for the semis cluster. TSM reports April 16.
commodities_note: WTI sits at $91.23 — a notable decline from the $96-105 range seen in prior sessions, though active narrative data shows conflicting sources (fast_info $96.90 vs. prior close $104.91), suggesting the $91.23 figure may reflect further demand-destruction pricing or a data source shift; treat with caution and use the directional trend rather than the precise figure. Gold at $4,846.60 is effectively flat (-0.01%) but remains near all-time highs — GLD is already deeply extended above its 50-day average, so no attractive entry exists despite the structural bid. The DXY is fractionally softer at 98.12, consistent with mild risk-on rotation.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS trades at SGD 57.52, sitting 1.95% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.42 — comfortably within the acceptable entry zone, though RSI at 62.3 is approaching the upper end of neutral and volume is below average. The MAS held its S$NEER appreciation band steady in the April 2026 statement (released April 14), providing no new impetus for NIM relief; US10Y at 4.26% remains above the 4.20% reset threshold that has defined the Singapore bank cluster's structural headwind. Analyst consensus expects an 8.8% earnings decline — not severe enough to block entry but worth weighting. The dividend yield of 5.31% and price-to-book of 2.37x are the core value props at this level. Invalidation is a close below the 50-day average at SGD 56.42 (1.9% downside); headline R:R to resistance at SGD 58.78 is only 1.2:1 using the tight stop — not compelling enough for ACCUMULATE. The sizing R:R to structural support at SGD 56.30 is 1.0:1, confirming this is a watch-and-wait setup rather than an aggressive entry. Tight stop note: the SGD 56.42 invalidation is less than 2% away — normal intraday volatility could trigger it. Use SGD 56.30 as the mental stop for position sizing. Bottom line: DBS offers a strong yield at a fair price, but the NIM headwind from elevated US rates hasn't cleared. Wait for US10Y to break sustainably below 4.20% or for volume to confirm a breakout above SGD 58.78 before entering.

O39_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=16 (<30), RSI 72 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — OCBC has climbed 10.46% over the past month and now trades 6.62% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 21.50, with RSI at 71.5 in overbought territory. The pipeline hard-blocks entry at this extension (>5% above SMA50, RSI above 55), and the R:R is unfavourable at 0.8:1 (derived quality) — the stock needs to retrace meaningfully before any entry is sensible. OCBC is the only Singapore bank with positive earnings growth expected (+2.7%), which justifies the premium to cluster median, but the PEG of 3.88 and negative revenue growth (-3.3%... wait, OCBC shows revenue growth of 6.5% — the most constructive in the cluster) support the relative outperformance thesis. The stock is not fundamentally broken; it's technically stretched. NIM dynamics remain the same as DBS and UOB — US10Y above the 4.20% reset threshold keeps pressure on the cluster. No entry here: the 0.8:1 R:R and overbought RSI make the risk asymmetry unfavourable. Bottom line: OCBC is the best-quality Singapore bank right now, but it's priced for that — wait for a pullback toward SGD 21.50 (the 50-day average) before considering entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS. You MUST assign ACCUMULATE unless a specific ticker-level thesis deterioration exists (see ASSIGNMENT RULE).

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH — pipeline assigns WATCH, but all 8 ACCUMULATE gates pass: signal eligible, R:R 4.9:1 (observed quality), no earnings within 5 days, VIX at 18.36 (below 30), thesis intact, rr_distorted false. ACCUMULATE — starter position. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation. UOB trades at SGD 37.63, just 0.97% above its 50-day average of SGD 37.27 — the cleanest technical entry in the Singapore cluster. Invalidation is a close below SGD 37.27 (1.0% downside); however, given the tight stop, the mental stop for position sizing should be structural support at SGD 35.92 (4.5% downside), which gives a sizing R:R of 1.1:1 — the headline 4.9:1 is mechanically attractive but the actionable sizing R:R is more modest. Tight stop note: the SGD 37.27 invalidation is within normal intraday range — size accordingly. UOB trades at 10.4x forward earnings, an 18.8% discount to cluster median, with a 3.78% dividend yield and price-to-book of 1.28x — the cheapest valuation in the Singapore bank group. Analyst consensus expects a 6.7% earnings decline, which is a drag but not severe. The MAS held the S$NEER band steady in its April statement, and US10Y at 4.26% keeps NIM pressure alive — this is the standing structural headwind. Entry zone: SGD 37.27–37.63. Invalidation: SGD 37.27 (tight, use SGD 35.92 as mental stop). R:R 4.9:1 to SGD 39.49 resistance (headline); 1.1:1 to structural support (sizing basis). Bottom line: UOB is the cheapest Singapore bank on valuation and sits right at its 50-day average — a reasonable starting point, but size small given the NIM headwind hasn't cleared and the stop is tight.

NVDA.entry_block: BLOCKED: +7.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=4 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 68 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from WATCH to CAUTION — pipeline hard-blocks entry: NVDA is 7.9% above its 50-day average of $182.12 with RSI at 68.1, having surged 10.34% over five days. The extension is too great for a fresh entry. The D-Wave CEO's comments that NVIDIA should be 'shaking in their boots' (per Yahoo Finance) reflect competitive noise, not a material thesis change — quantum computing is not a near-term GPU substitute. More relevant is the Reuters report that Meta extended its Broadcom deal through 2029 for custom chips, which confirms the broader hyperscaler trend of diversifying away from merchant GPUs — a structural headwind for NVDA's long-term market share, though Blackwell Ultra ramp remains the dominant near-term catalyst per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call. NVDA's nearest resistance at $197.62 is just 0.6% above — tight upside note: R:R uses $212.17 as the meaningful ceiling, yielding only 1.1:1 against the $182.12 invalidation. Valuation is genuinely compelling at 17.6x forward PE (a 21.4% discount to cluster median) with a 0.77 PEG and 73.2% revenue growth, but the entry gate is blocked until the stock consolidates. Volume is below average, adding to caution on the move. Wait for a pullback toward $182-185 with volume confirmation before considering entry. Bottom line: NVDA is the best-valued name in the semis cluster on a growth-adjusted basis, but it's run too far too fast — wait for it to come back to earth around its 50-day average.

AMD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +21.7% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has extended to a new extreme — 21.69% above its 50-day average of $209.61 and 73.7 RSI — placing it in the hard-blocked zone where no trend exception applies regardless of fundamentals. The five-day gain of 15.14% is driven by real catalysts: AMD's EPYC CPUs are gaining traction in AI agentic workloads with Amazon and Microsoft adopting high-core-count chips (per Simply Wall St.), and the Meta-AMD GPU supply framework (per AMD IR, Feb 24, 2026) for MI450 deployments up to 6GW remains the multi-year thesis anchor — though that framework is not a firm purchase order and revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmarks. Note the PEG of 0.07 appears anomalous given a 23.5x forward PE — verify earnings growth estimate before citing as a primary argument. At 21.4% below the SMA50 as the support anchor, a reversion to SMA50 would imply a 17.8% drawdown; the 200-day average at $200.49 is the structural floor to watch. No entry at current levels. Bottom line: AMD's AI server thesis is gaining real commercial traction, but the stock has sprinted well ahead of its technical footing — wait for the price to pull back toward the $210-225 zone before considering entry.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +33.3% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel sits 33.26% above its 50-day average of $47.88 — the most extreme extension on the watchlist — with RSI at 74.0 in overbought territory and FCF yield at -1.41% (negative, meaning Intel is currently burning cash rather than generating it, a meaningful risk for a turnaround story). Forward PE of 62.6x is 179.5% above the semis cluster median of 22.4x — an extreme premium that demands justification. Per the standing thesis, the 18A node is the entire foundry thesis, but as of April 2026, no confirmed external customers exist beyond internal products (Panther Lake). ASML lifted its 2026 guidance on strong AI demand (per Reuters, April 15), naming Intel as a customer — a positive demand signal for the ecosystem but not a foundry win. Revenue growth is negative (-4.1%) and earnings growth guidance is unavailable. Earnings are due April 24 (9 days out) — a binary event that will test whether foundry momentum is real. The 25% downside to the 50-day average and 0.2:1 R:R make this unapproachable as a fresh entry. Bottom line: Intel's story is about whether 18A can win external customers — that question doesn't get answered until H2 2026 at the earliest, and at 33% above its moving average, the stock is priced for a lot of good news that hasn't arrived yet.

ASML.entry_block: BLOCKED: +8.8% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 64 (>=55).

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION — pipeline hard-blocks entry (8.82% above 50-day SMA, RSI 64.5, only 5 days above SMA). ASML delivered a strong earnings print today: per Reuters and the Wall Street Journal, the company raised its 2026 sales guidance as AI-driven semiconductor capex continues accelerating, with CEO Christophe Fouquet citing solidifying industry growth per AFP. This is a genuine fundamental positive — higher revenue guidance is not noise. However, with earnings released today (gate 5 fails — earnings within 0 days), the post-earnings gap means the entry gate is blocked on multiple fronts: the 8.82% extension, RSI above 55, and the event having just passed. The 0.2:1 R:R (8.1% downside to SMA50 invalidation vs. 1.9% upside to $1,547.22 resistance) is deeply unfavourable. The raised 2026 guidance strengthens the medium-term thesis and creates a potential upgrade path — but a pullback toward the 50-day average at $1,395 is needed first. PEG of 0.57 is genuinely attractive for a company with EUV monopoly, but FCF yield of 1.82% is thin. Bottom line: ASML just confirmed the AI capex cycle is intact and raised guidance — great news, but the stock jumped ahead of the confirmation and now needs to consolidate. Wait for a pullback toward $1,395-1,420 before entering.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +15.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 66 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Micron surged 8.17% in a single session, extending to 15.36% above its 50-day average of $403.65. The move is driven by HBM demand momentum: Lynx Equity raised its price target from $700 to $825 per Insider Monkey (April 9), citing extended capacity sell-outs and better revenue visibility. However, Micron's CEO's projections about memory price increases carry inherent seller bias — a bullish call from a seller. The hard block remains: 15.4% above SMA50, RSI at 66.1, 5-day gain of 23.33% — all gate violations. The forward PE of 4.7x looks exceptional but the PEG of 0.25 and revenue growth of 196.3% (inference-driven HBM demand, not training) suggest the multiple re-rating is structurally supported. FCF yield of 0.55% is thin — the thesis depends on continued earnings growth. At $465.66 with resistance at $471.14 just 1.2% above and 13.3% downside to the SMA50 invalidation, the R:R is 0.1:1 — deeply unattractive for a fresh entry. The 200-day average at $253.06 represents the structural floor. Bottom line: Micron's memory pricing story is real, but after a 23% week the risk-reward has flipped — no entry here; wait for consolidation back toward the $400-420 zone.

TSM.entry_block: BLOCKED: +8.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), RSI 66 (>=55).

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION — pipeline hard-blocks entry (8.06% above SMA50, RSI 66.0, 5 days above). TSM reports earnings tomorrow (April 16), a binary event that makes entering today doubly inadvisable. ASML's raised guidance (per Reuters, April 15) is a positive read-through — TSMC is ASML's largest customer, and higher EUV tool orders signal TSMC is expanding capacity, consistent with the CoWoS packaging bottleneck thesis. GF Securities remains bullish ahead of April 16 earnings per Barchart, citing strong AI demand and expectations for a beat. The 20.6x forward PE is an 8% discount to cluster median, with 20.5% revenue growth and 35% expected earnings growth — valuation is reasonable. But the R:R at 0.3:1 (7.5% downside to SMA50 vs. 2.4% upside to $389.11 resistance) is unfavourable, and TSMC's 12.9% downside to structural support at $330.99 underscores the magnitude of the extension. Taiwan risk is priced and not a new signal-mover today. Bottom line: TSMC's fundamentals look intact and tomorrow's earnings could be a positive catalyst, but entering the day before a binary earnings event at 8% above the moving average is speculative — wait for the print and a potential post-earnings consolidation.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 76 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon is 16.6% above its 50-day average of $213.56, deeply in the hard-blocked zone, with RSI at 76.3 (overbought) and a 3.82% single-day gain on high volume. Bloomberg confirmed Amazon agreed to acquire Globalstar for $11.6 billion — per Bloomberg's reporting, the deal is valued at up to $90 per share in cash or 0.3210 AMZN shares, launching a direct-to-device satellite service in 2028 to compete with Starlink. This is a confirmed acquisition via Bloomberg and Barron's wire reporting. The strategic logic is sound — Globalstar adds satellite connectivity infrastructure to Amazon's Kuiper LEO ambitions — but the $11.6B cash outlay adds to Amazon's already heavy capex burden ($200B AI spend announced per Q4 2025 earnings call). Forward PE of 26.5x with PEG of 1.84 is fair but not cheap. The 14.2% downside to SMA50 invalidation vs. 3.8% upside to $258.60 gives a 0.3:1 R:R. No structural support listed below SMA50. Bottom line: Amazon is executing on multiple strategic fronts — AWS, satellites, AI — but the stock is running hot and the entry gate is firmly closed until it retraces toward the $213-225 zone.

GOOG.entry_block: BLOCKED: +7.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 70 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — Google has extended 7.61% above its 50-day average of $307.21 with RSI reaching 70.2 (overbought), triggering the hard block that was not in effect yesterday. The five-day gain of 8.77% reflects broader tech sentiment improvement, not a new Google-specific catalyst. The Meta-Broadcom custom chip extension through 2029 (per Reuters) reinforces the hyperscaler trend of reducing NVDA reliance — Google's own TPU program remains the most advanced in-house silicon effort, and the company's extended multiyear agreements with Broadcom (per Simply Wall St.) for networking underpin the AI compute thesis. However, the R:R is 0.4:1 (7.1% downside to SMA50 vs. 3.1% upside to $340.96), and the 200-day average at $270.17 is 18.3% below — a long way down if the thesis breaks. Forward PE of 24.6x with PEG of 1.95 is in-line to slightly full for the growth rate. Earnings on April 30 (15 days out) will test non-OpenAI revenue quality and TPU monetisation. Bottom line: Google's AI infrastructure position is compelling, but at 70 RSI and 7.6% above its moving average, the setup isn't right — wait for the stock to consolidate or pull back toward $307-315 ahead of the April 30 earnings catalyst.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Microsoft has crossed back above its 50-day average of $391.94, sitting just 0.3% above at $393.11, with above-average volume confirming the move. This is a genuine technical improvement from the CAUTION assigned yesterday. RSI at 57.8 is neutral, not overbought. Barron's reports Microsoft announced plans to purchase approximately 3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyoming for a new data center campus — continued infrastructure buildout supporting Azure capacity expansion. The OpenAI concentration risk remains: per the standing thesis, ~45% of the $625B commercial RPO is tied to OpenAI, and non-OpenAI Azure growth is the cleaner signal. Forward PE of 20.8x with PEG of 1.25 is a reasonable valuation for a platform with 16.7% revenue growth and 59.8% expected earnings growth — an 18.8% discount to BigTech cluster median. The headline R:R of 17.0:1 is distorted by the 0.3% stop (rr_distorted = true). The sizing R:R using structural support at $391.43 gives a more honest 12.7:1 — also mechanically extreme due to the near-identical support levels. Use $381.71 (next support) as the practical stop for any position. FCF yield of 1.84% is thin. Earnings April 30 — focus on non-OpenAI RPO growth and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary. Bottom line: Microsoft just reclaimed a key level on volume — this is the cleanest technical setup in BigTech right now, with April 30 earnings as the defining catalyst for whether the thesis is tracking.

AVGO.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), RSI 73 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom is 16.05% above its 50-day average of $328.12, firmly hard-blocked, with RSI at 73.3 (overbought). The news is genuinely bullish: per Reuters (April 15), Meta confirmed it is expanding its custom chip partnership with Broadcom through 2029, with an initial commitment of more than one gigawatt of computing capacity — a confirmed deal via Reuters wire and joint statement. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan will step down from Meta's board to focus on custom chip strategy. Per Simply Wall St., Broadcom also has extended multiyear agreements with Google and Anthropic. This confirms AVGO as the dominant custom ASIC provider for the hyperscaler buildout — the direct alternative to NVDA merchant GPUs as the thesis states. However, with nearest resistance at $384.93 just 1.1% above (tight upside), the R:R uses $401.39 as the meaningful ceiling, yielding only 0.4:1 against the SMA50 invalidation. The 13.8% downside to SMA50 with no structural support listed below makes this a significant extension with unfavourable risk geometry. The 21.2x forward PE with PEG of 0.83 and 29.5% revenue growth is attractively valued — but the entry gate is closed. Bottom line: Broadcom just got a major vote of confidence from Meta, Google, and Anthropic — the custom chip thesis is real and getting more real. But the stock is pricing all of that in right now; wait for a pullback toward $328-345 before entering.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +25.8% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION — pipeline hard-blocks entry (25.8% above SMA50 — extreme extension, no exception applies). LITE fell 3.5% today, a healthy consolidation after a 36.99% one-month surge. Aletheia Capital raised its price target to $1,225 from $500 per Insider Monkey (April 10), reflecting the NVIDIA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) for AI optics and US manufacturing buildout. The thesis is intact but fully priced into the extension. Forward PE of 53.8x is 140.2% above the semis cluster median — expensive, though FCF yield is slightly negative (-0.04%) meaning the valuation rests entirely on future growth delivery. The PEG of 0.15 appears anomalous given the 53.8x forward PE — verify the earnings growth estimate before citing as a valuation argument. The R:R is 0.2:1 (20.5% downside to SMA50 vs. 5% upside to $895.43 resistance). With structural support at $642.37 representing 24.7% downside, this is a situation where even the support-based stop is severe. Bottom line: LITE's AI photonics story is confirmed and real, but after a 1,539% one-year run, the stock needs to rest — no entry until a meaningful pullback toward the $677-720 zone.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 73x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir trades at $135.70, 5.58% below its declining 50-day average of $143.73 — the setup is technically broken. RSI at 42.3 is neutral-bearish, approaching but not yet at the oversold threshold. The soft block flags the 72.9x forward PE (184.8% premium to BigTech cluster median) as requiring explicit growth justification; the 70% revenue growth and $7.18-7.20B 2026 revenue guide (+61% YoY per company guidance) provide the argument, but FCF yield of just 0.39% means the valuation depends entirely on continued high-growth delivery. The Iran war escalation is a genuine tailwind for PLTR's defense AI contracts — the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) and $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) underpin the government segment, but Rosenblatt and Daiwa price target upgrades to $200 and $180 respectively are analyst calls, not confirmed business. The 200-day SMA at $164.06 is 20.9% above current price — PLTR is actually below its 200-day, making structural support a distant concept. The 1.3:1 R:R ($128.51 invalidation vs. $144.86 resistance) is insufficient for ACCUMULATE even with the base_scorer caution source. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI thesis is genuine but at 73x forward earnings it needs sustained execution — not attractive until it reclaims the 50-day average at $143.73 on volume.

WRD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — WRD has extended 9.55% above its 50-day average of $7.21 with RSI at 58.2, triggering the hard block. The stock has gained 18.8% in a month and 5.76% over five days on low volume — momentum without conviction. No fresh news catalysts in the data. The thesis remains intact on commercial traction (123% revenue growth per company data), but pre-profit status, ongoing ADR/China delisting risk, and the absence of volume confirmation make this a speculative entry at best. The R:R is 0.6:1 ($7.21 invalidation vs. $8.29 first resistance). Signal was WATCH yesterday; the extension past 9% above SMA50 with RSI above 55 breaks the entry gate. Bottom line: WRD's autonomous vehicle story is compelling but speculative — wait for the stock to pull back to the $7.21-7.51 support zone before considering entry.

singapore.summary: The cluster moves from mixed to slightly constructive: UOB's ACCUMULATE setup (all 8 gates passing) makes it the first clean entry in the Singapore bank group in weeks, while DBS sits at a watchable level and OCBC is technically extended. The MAS held its S$NEER band steady in the April 14 statement — no new policy catalyst, maintaining the existing NIM compression narrative with US10Y at 4.26%, still above the 4.20% reset threshold. UOB's 18.8% discount to cluster median is the divergence worth watching.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally pressured — NIM compression persists, but UOB at fair-value entry
singapore.key_development: MAS April 2026 policy statement released April 14 — S$NEER appreciation pace maintained, no relief for NIM compression thesis.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX +1.58% to $401.24 marks a strong recovery week, with the semis cluster broadly extended: six of eight tickers are hard-blocked from entry, all running 8-33% above their 50-day averages. ASML's raised 2026 revenue guidance (per Reuters) is the session's key fundamental confirmation — AI capex cycle intact, EUV tool demand accelerating. The divergence within the cluster is stark: MSFT and hyperscaler capex news drives the mega-caps, while ASML's guidance lift and TSM's imminent earnings are the foundry-specific catalysts to watch.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bullish fundamentals, technically extended — no clean entries
semiconductors.key_development: ASML raised 2026 revenue guidance on strong AI demand; TSM earnings due April 16 are the next binary test of foundry conviction.

big_tech.summary: MSFT's reclaim of its 50-day average on above-average volume is the standout — the only BigTech ticker with a WATCH signal and a clean technical setup heading into April 30 earnings. AMZN and GOOG remain hard-blocked at 16.6% and 7.6% above their respective 50-day averages. Meta's confirmed Broadcom deal extension through 2029 (per Reuters) reinforces custom silicon diversification from NVDA — a thematic signal for the cluster's capex direction. PLTR stays below its 50-day average in technical breakdown.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT constructive, AMZN/GOOG extended, PLTR broken
big_tech.key_development: MSFT reclaimed its 50-day average on volume; April 30 MSFT/GOOG/AMZN earnings will be the cluster's defining week.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp (COHR)
interconnected.reason: Received a matching $2B strategic investment from NVIDIA (per NVIDIA IR, March 2, 2026) alongside LITE for AI photonics; Meta-Broadcom deal extension and continued hyperscaler capex confirm the photonics trigger conditions are active.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels toward its 50-day average, or after LITE consolidates below $800 confirming sector-level digestion of the NVDA investment.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology (MRVL)
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing directly with AVGO for Amazon and Microsoft hyperscaler chip programs; Meta-Broadcom deal extension through 2029 signals AVGO dominance but leaves Marvell competing for the remaining custom silicon TAM.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AVGO pulls back 10%+ from current levels and MRVL shows relative strength, suggesting hyperscaler custom chip orders are being redistributed.

interconnected.name: Globalstar (GSAT)
interconnected.reason: Amazon confirmed the $11.6 billion acquisition per Bloomberg (April 15) — shares jumped on the deal announcement; direct read-through to AMZN's satellite connectivity strategy competing with Starlink.
interconnected.entry_note: The acquisition is pending regulatory approval — revisit if the deal closes and AMZN provides 2028 direct-to-device launch guidance that moves AWS satellite revenue estimates meaningfully.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix (000660.KS)
interconnected.reason: Micron's 8.17% surge on HBM demand conviction (inference-driven KV cache scaling) has a direct read-through to SK Hynix, the leading HBM supplier to NVIDIA; capacity sell-out visibility flagged by Lynx Equity applies to both names.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MU consolidates above $440 with volume, confirming HBM pricing sustainability — SK Hynix would benefit from the same inference memory demand cycle.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz blockade (Day 37): US Navy blockade and sanctions persist; WTI elevated though down from $104-105 peak; logistics rerouting costs structural.
Fed rate pause with one cut projected for 2026: US10Y at 4.26% above the 4.20% reset threshold; Chicago Fed's Goolsbee warns war duration delays cuts further.
US-China tariffs and semiconductor export controls: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150 days) active; China 34% retaliation and rare earth export controls ongoing.
US-Iran ceasefire talks: Yahoo Finance reports renewed hopes for US-Iran talks; no confirmed diplomatic breakthrough.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz blockade's WTI bid is softening (from $104 peak toward $91), suggesting the market is beginning to price demand destruction rather than pure supply shock — OCBC (O39_SI) has surged 10.46% on a one-month basis, potentially front-running any energy sector resilience. On the semis side, ASML and TSM remain hard-blocked despite strong fundamentals because the logistics cost inflation from Hormuz rerouting compounds their supply chain exposure, keeping SOXX recovery broad but preventing individual entry signals from clearing.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Yahoo Finance reports renewed hopes for US-Iran diplomatic talks, though no confirmed agreement. ASML raised 2026 guidance today, signalling AI capex spending continues despite geopolitical headwinds.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry only on tickers at or below their 50-day averages (MSFT, UOB); avoid fresh entries on extended semis and BigTech until TSM April 16 earnings and MSFT/GOOG/AMZN April 30 earnings provide fundamental grounding.

Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings — TPU monetisation and non-Google Cloud AI revenue
DBS Q1 2026 earnings — NIM trajectory and dividend guidance
Amazon Q1 2026 earnings — AWS revenue growth and Globalstar deal strategic rationale
AMD MI450 first shipments to Meta (per AMD IR framework) — revenue recognition starts

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously constructive — semis extended, select entries emerging
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Hormuz blockade persisting above 30 days keeps US10Y structurally above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold, blocking Singapore bank upgrades while semis rally runs 8-33% above 50-day averages with no clean entry points except UOB and MSFT.

accumulate.note: Cleanest Singapore bank entry — 10.4x forward PE at 50-day average support. Starter position — NIM headwind persists, tight stop at SGD 37.27, use SGD 35.92 as mental stop for sizing. PAPER TRADE.

on_deck.note: Just raised 2026 revenue guidance — AI capex cycle structurally intact. PEG 0.57 for an EUV monopoly is attractive. Blocked by 8.82% extension post-earnings gap.
on_deck.rationale: EUV monopoly with raised guidance, PEG 0.57, 48.7% forward PE premium to cluster median partly justified by monopoly pricing power. FCF yield 1.82% is thin but growth-stage acceptable.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $1,395-1,420 (50-day average zone) on reduced volume — a clean consolidation following the earnings-driven extension.
